assessment.recommendation: Do not change the dependency: the Observatory already matches the latest stable release. Keep Codex-derived totals and API-equivalent cost visibly qualified for fork/subagent-heavy histories, watch issue #1434 and the unmerged fix in PR #1435, and reassess when a runtime release lands.
assessment.summary: The project is exactly current at ccusage 20.0.17 and its consumed JSON contracts validate. Six commits exist after the release, but all are build, CI, Nix, benchmark, fixture-generation, or release-process work. Separately, upstream has a reproducible Codex fork/subagent replay overcount in 20.0.17 and current main; the proposed runtime fix is not merged.

sources.label: ccusage repository
sources.note: Canonical upstream source and current feature documentation.

sources.label: v20.0.17 to main comparison
sources.note: Six post-release commits; no adapter or report-contract runtime changes.

unreleased_changes.title: Six post-release commits affect development and release tooling only
unreleased_changes.summary: Observed upstream changes cover Nix dependency caching, a Babashka benchmark harness, Bun fixture generation, CI cache maintenance, dev-shell worktree setup, and tagpr release triggers.
unreleased_changes.impact: No Observatory runtime, CLI flag, JSON report, pricing, adapter, or session-identity change is visible in the comparison. This is an observed file-level result, not a prediction about a future release.

opportunities.title: Extend metadata-only project enrichment beyond Claude and Codex
opportunities.value: Unified ccusage reports already ingest 15 supported agent sources, but Observatory project paths, stable session enrichment, and project activity currently cover only Claude and Codex. Adding selected high-value sources would turn existing aggregate rows into project-level analytics.
opportunities.fit: Fits the local-only architecture if each added source gets an explicit metadata-head parser, stable native-key mapping, and regression fixtures without copying prompt or response content.
required_work: Choose sources based on actual local usage rather than implementing all agents at once.
Add source-specific path discovery and native session-key extraction in server/path-indexer.ts.
Expand provider/project types and identity fixtures while preserving annotation continuity.
Surface unsupported enrichment explicitly instead of presenting missing paths as collection failure.

opportunities.title: Expose an explicit reporting timezone
opportunities.value: ccusage supports IANA timezones, while the Observatory currently accepts the process/system timezone implicitly. An explicit setting would make day, week, and month boundaries deterministic when the app moves between machines or environments.
opportunities.fit: This remains local and can be passed as a CLI argument alongside --offline. The same setting must also drive Observatory-side local-date grouping to avoid mixed boundaries.
required_work: Add and validate an IANA timezone setting.
Pass --timezone to unified and block commands.
Use the same timezone in project aggregation and document the boundary.

opportunities.title: Add governed local pricing overrides for newly released models
opportunities.value: Upstream configuration supports runtime pricing overrides keyed by raw model name. A controlled override could prevent temporary zero or stale API-equivalent costs while bundled offline pricing catches up.
opportunities.fit: Compatible with offline operation, but the Observatory must label overrides separately, record their source and effective date, and avoid implying that user-supplied values are upstream ccusage defaults.
required_work: Use an Observatory-owned explicit config path instead of relying on ambient auto-discovery.
Validate model names and numeric pricing fields against the bundled schema.
Expose override provenance in methodology and source health.
Add cost regression fixtures before enabling an override.

opportunities.title: Codex five-hour block reporting
opportunities.value: A Codex-focused block report could complement the existing Claude-only locally reconstructed blocks and make the block scope less asymmetric.
opportunities.fit: The installed CLI has no `codex blocks` command. Treat this as a watch item, not an available integration. If upstream ships it, its reset-based grouping must remain distinct from provider-reported quota-service allowance data.
required_work: Wait for an upstream runtime implementation and documented JSON contract.
Add a separate schema and source label; do not merge it with authoritative provider quota.
Validate reset timestamps and cross-session grouping against fixtures.

opportunities.title: Codex usage by reasoning effort
opportunities.value: Reasoning-effort attribution could make the Observatory's model-efficiency views more useful by distinguishing low, medium, high, xhigh, and max usage within a model.
opportunities.fit: The proposed effortBreakdown JSON is not in 20.0.17 or main. Wait for an upstream contract before adding product/UI dependencies; a local prototype would require raw event interpretation outside the current ccusage boundary.
required_work: Monitor for a merged upstream parser and stable JSON field.
Add optional schema support so older rows remain valid.
Define how effort affects charts without implying model quality.

issues.title: Significant accounting issues for Codex
issues.relevance: Applies to 20.0.17. Fork and subagent logs can replay parent token history, causing potentially material overcount in token and API-equivalent cost totals. The Observatory consumes those totals directly.

issues.title: Codex fork replays spanning multiple seconds are counted as native usage
issues.relevance: Provides a minimized reproduction against both 20.0.17 and current Rust HEAD. Closure does not indicate a shipped fix; the current main comparison contains no Codex parser change.

issues.title: Add ccusage codex blocks
issues.relevance: Potential Observatory feature, but the installed CLI help confirms that Codex currently exposes daily, monthly, and session commands only.

issues.title: Add Codex usage breakdown by reasoning effort
issues.relevance: Potential input for model-efficiency analytics, but no effortBreakdown contract is available in stable or current main.

local_surface.role: Exact dependency pin and platform binary resolution
local_surface.notes: Both resolve 20.0.17 and agree with the installed binary.

local_surface.role: CLI invocation boundary for unified multi-section and Claude block JSON
local_surface.notes: Flags and top-level output shapes are compatibility-sensitive; current commands validate at 20.0.17.

local_surface.role: Zod boundary for unified rows, per-agent/model breakdowns, totals, and blocks
local_surface.notes: Current structural samples match. Optional upstream additions are stripped unless the schema intentionally preserves them.

local_surface.role: Normalizes sessions, models, project activity, and displayed totals
local_surface.notes: It trusts ccusage totals; upstream Codex replay overcount therefore propagates to charts, projects, and API-equivalent cost.

local_surface.role: Metadata-only Claude/Codex path discovery and stable session identity
local_surface.notes: Any new agent source or upstream native-key change requires an explicit parser and migration-safe compatibility mapping.

local_surface.role: Normalized contract and stable identity regression coverage
local_surface.notes: Covers synthetic shapes and project aggregation, but not pinned real-output fixtures or fork-replay accounting.

local_surface.role: Privacy, attribution, methodology, and identity guarantees
local_surface.notes: Future upgrades must preserve local-only collection and distinguish reconstructed activity from provider quota.

local_surface.role: Displays version, source attribution, sessions, models, projects, and block scope
local_surface.notes: New agents or effort/block dimensions would require type and presentation decisions, not only ingestion changes.

validation.check: Pinned, locked, and installed version agreement

validation.notes: Six commits ahead; changed files are limited to build, CI, Nix, benchmark, fixture, and release tooling.
Top-level sections and daily, session, agent, model, and totals keys match the local schemas; only key names and counts were retained.
The block array and consumed fields match the local schema; only key names and counts were retained.

limitations: No standalone CHANGELOG or CHANGES file exists in the current upstream tree; GitHub release notes and the exact comparison view were used instead.
The audit did not inspect raw Codex or Claude transcript content and did not quantify whether this machine's history contains fork/subagent replay patterns.
Upstream issues #1431, #1452, and #1460 were closed immediately by contribution automation; their closed state does not mean the requested feature or fix shipped.
The working tree contains unrelated uncommitted application changes. The audit assessed the current working-tree integration and did not modify those files.
